issuesFound.title: Small base font size
issuesFound.explanation: The base font size is set to 12px on the html element. This makes the default text size for the entire page too small for many users, particularly those with visual impairments, and falls below the recommended 16px standard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 139: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element uses a negative word-spacing value of -0.2em. This reduces the space between words, making text harder to distinguish and read for users with low vision or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 166: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element uses a negative letter-spacing value of -0.05em. Tightening the space between characters can cause letters to overlap or blur together, significantly hindering readability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 167: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line-height below minimum
issuesFound.explanation: Paragraph elements have a line-height of 1. This fails to meet the minimum requirement of 1.5 for body text, which is necessary to ensure sufficient vertical space between lines for readability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 188: line-height: 1.5;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: Underlines are removed from all anchor elements globally. Without underlines, links within blocks of text rely solely on color to be identified, which is a failure for users with color vision deficiency.
issuesFound.fix: Line 201: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline to none without defining a visible replacement style like a solid outline or box-shadow. This prevents keyboard users from knowing which element currently has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 264: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto. This implementation fails to visually hide the content, causing screen-reader-only text to appear on the screen for all users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 295: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-text-secondary variable (#aaaaaa) has a contrast ratio of 2.32:1 against the white background (#ffffff). This is well below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 68: --colour-text-secondary: #6b6b6b;

issuesFound.explanation: The current implementation of the visually-hidden utility uses position: static and width: auto, which keeps the content visible on the screen. This defeats the purpose of a screen-reader-only class.

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions update CSS classes and scroll locking but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the menu state has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1045: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` Line 1059: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels but does not update the aria-pressed state on the trigger buttons. This prevents users of assistive technology from perceiving the toggle state of the menu buttons.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu initialization script registers triggers and panels but does not explicitly set the initial aria-expanded="false" state on the buttons. Assistive technologies may not correctly announce the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1285: `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The sticky header hide and show functions emit custom events and change CSS classes but do not provide a non-visual announcement. Users with visual impairments may not realize the header has been hidden or revealed during scroll.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1355: `liveRegion.announce('Header hidden');` Line 1363: `liveRegion.announce('Header visible');`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validator's _renderResult function updates the error message text and visibility but does not set the aria-invalid attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not announce the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2905: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, !result.valid);`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The clearField function in the inline validator removes error classes and hides the error element but fails to reset or remove the aria-invalid attribute. A previously invalid field will continue to be announced as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2945: `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global selector `:focus-visible` removes the default focus outline (`outline: none`) but does not provide any alternative visible focus style such as a box‑shadow or colored outline. Keyboard‑only users lose the visual cue that indicates which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300: `:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); /* add a visible focus style, e.g.: box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); */ }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `:focus:not(:focus-visible)` also removes the outline without supplying an alternative, causing a focus loss for users navigating with a keyboard when the element is focused but not focus‑visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 304: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; /* add a visible focus style, e.g.: box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); */ }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons lose their focus outline (`outline: none`) and no replacement style is defined, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which button is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; /* add visible focus style, e.g.: box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); */ }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes its outline on focus (`outline: none`) without providing an alternative visual cue, breaking focus visibility for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 230: `.nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; /* add visible focus style, e.g.: box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); */ }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter‑tab buttons hide the focus outline (`outline: none`) and do not supply any other visible focus indicator, preventing keyboard users from tracking focus within the tab list.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: `.filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; /* add visible focus style, e.g.: box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); */ }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove the outline on focus (`outline: none`) without any replacement, so users navigating with a keyboard cannot see which question is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: -3px; /* add visible focus style, e.g.: box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); */ }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes its outline on focus (`outline: none`) and provides no alternative visual focus indicator, making keyboard navigation of the table region invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 880: `.table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; /* add visible focus style, e.g.: box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); */ }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn` has a `min-height: 32px` (total height ≈ 44 px after padding) but the vertical padding is only 6 px, which is less than the recommended 8 px compensation. The effective touch target may be smaller than the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 44 × 44 px, affecting users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: `.btn { min-height: 32px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-5); /* increase vertical padding to at least 8px, e.g.: padding-top: var(--space-3); padding-bottom: var(--space-3); */ }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-sm` sets `min-height: 20px`, which is well below the 44 px minimum touch target size, making the button difficult to activate for users with limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380: `.btn-sm { min-height: 20px; /* increase to at least 44px, e.g.: min-height: 44px; padding-top: var(--space-3); padding-bottom: var(--space-3); */ }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-icon` defines `min-width: 20px; min-height: 20px`, which is smaller than the WCAG‑recommended 44 px touch target, hindering users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 400: `.btn-icon { min-width: 20px; min-height: 20px; /* increase to at least 44px, e.g.: min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; */ }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: Filter‑tab buttons have `min-height: 28px` with only 6 px vertical padding, resulting in a total height of about 40 px, which is below the 44 px minimum and lacks sufficient padding compensation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: `.filter-tabs button { min-height: 28px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-5); /* increase vertical padding to ≥8px, e.g.: padding-top: var(--space-3); padding-bottom: var(--space-3); */ }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle buttons use `min-height: 28px` with only 6 px vertical padding, giving a total height of ~40 px, which is below the WCAG‑recommended 44 px touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: `.pricing-toggle button { min-height: 28px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-5); /* increase vertical padding to ≥8px, e.g.: padding-top: var(--space-3); padding-bottom: var(--space-3); */ }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social‑link anchors have `min-height: 28px` with only 6 px vertical padding, resulting in an effective height of ~40 px, which does not meet the 44 px minimum for touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 960: `.social-links a { min-height: 28px; padding-block: var(--space-2); /* increase vertical padding to ≥8px, e.g.: padding-top: var(--space-3); padding-bottom: var(--space-3); */ }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links are given `min-height: 24px` with no additional padding, resulting in a touch target smaller than the 44 px minimum, which can be problematic for users with limited motor control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1020: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { min-height: 24px; /* increase to at least 44px, e.g.: min-height: 44px; padding-top: var(--space-3); padding-bottom: var(--space-3); */ }`

issuesFound.title: Visually‑hidden utility removes content from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class sets `position: static; width: auto; height: auto; clip: auto;` which does **not** hide the element from the visual layout nor from assistive technologies. This defeats the purpose of a screen‑reader‑only utility and can expose hidden content to sighted users or hide it from AT if later overridden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 250: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn` defines a transition that includes `transform`, creating movement that is not suppressed for users who prefer reduced motion, and there is no corresponding `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule to disable it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: `.btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-image img` animates `transform` and `box-shadow` on hover, but no reduced‑motion media query disables these animations for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 460: `.hero-image img { transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: `.feature-card` applies a transition that includes `transform` for the hover lift effect, without a reduced‑motion override, causing unwanted motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 520: `.feature-card { transition: border-color var(--transition-base), box-shadow var(--transition-base), transform var(--transition-base); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: `.pricing-card` includes a transition that animates `transform` on hover, but no reduced‑motion media query disables this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 660: `.pricing-card { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base), transform var(--transition-base); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: `.product-card` defines a hover `transform` with a transition that includes `transform`, yet there is no reduced‑motion guard, causing motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 620: `.product-card { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base), transform var(--transition-base); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .stat-item
issuesFound.explanation: `.stat-item` animates `transform` on hover (lift effect) without a reduced‑motion override, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users preferring reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: `.stat-item { transition: background-color var(--transition-base), transform var(--transition-base); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stat-item { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative styling
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes the default underline from all links, and no other non‑colour visual differentiator (e.g., border‑bottom, background highlight) is provided, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text for users relying on visual cues.
issuesFound.fix: Line 140: `a { text-decoration: none; /* add alternative visual cue, e.g.: border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; */ }`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger lacks initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init()` the trigger button is wired for clicks and key events, but no `aria‑expanded` attribute is set to reflect the initial closed state. Screen readers therefore receive no information about the menu’s default state.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not update aria‑pressed when toggled
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period selector buttons (`[data-period]`) are visually toggled with the `CSS.active` class, but the ARIA state `aria‑pressed` is never set. Users of assistive technology cannot know which period is active.

issuesFound.title: Inline field validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator._renderResult()` marks fields as valid/invalid only by adding CSS classes and managing error messages. It never sets the `aria‑invalid` attribute, so screen readers are not informed when a field contains an error.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button (single‑button switch) does not set aria‑pressed on its state change
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off billing toggle (`[data-billing-toggle]`) updates a visual “checked” class on its inner track but never updates `aria‑pressed`, leaving the accessible state undefined.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button lacks initial aria‑pressed attribute on page load
issuesFound.explanation: In `pricing.init()` the toggle element is created but no `aria‑pressed` attribute reflects its initial state (month vs. year). Assistive technologies cannot infer the default selection.

Pricing period toggle button (single‑button switch) does not set aria‑pressed on its state change

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button (`_trigger` selected via `SELECTORS.navToggle`) opens and closes the navigation drawer, but the script never sets or updates the `aria-expanded` attribute on the button to reflect the drawer’s state. This prevents screen‑reader users from knowing whether the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: In the `open()` and `close()` functions (around line 1150), add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger button missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items use a button (`trigger`) to expand/collapse the answer panel, but the code never toggles the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger when the item is opened or closed. This leaves assistive technology unaware of the panel’s visibility.
issuesFound.fix: In the `open(item)` and `close(item)` functions (around line 2600), add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Pricing billing‑period toggle switch missing aria‑checked/aria‑pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (`_toggleEl`) is given `role="switch"` but the script never updates its state via `aria-checked` (or `aria-pressed`). Users of assistive technology cannot tell whether the switch is on (annual) or off (monthly).
issuesFound.fix: In the `_syncToggleState(period)` function (around line 3000), after determining the active period, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false')` (or `aria-pressed` if preferred) to keep the ARIA state in sync.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger buttons missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are given `aria-haspopup="true"` but the script never sets `aria-expanded` to indicate whether the associated panel is currently open. This omission hides the expanded/collapsed state from screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In the `_openInstance(inst)` and `_closeInstance(inst)` functions (around line 3400), add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing required ARIA state
issuesFound.explanation: The element selected as the mobile‑nav toggle (`_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle)`) is used to open/close the off‑canvas navigation drawer, but the script never sets the ARIA attributes that convey its state to assistive technologies. The button lacks `aria-controls` (pointing to the drawer) and `aria-expanded` (reflecting open/closed state). Without these, screen‑reader users cannot know that the button controls a navigation region or whether it is currently expanded.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing required `aria‑expanded` attribute on load
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ parser creates the relationship between each trigger button and its answer panel (`setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);` on line 2270) but does not set an initial `aria-expanded` value on the trigger. According to the ARIA accordion pattern, every trigger must have `aria-expanded="false"` (or `"true"` if it starts open) so that screen‑reader users can perceive the collapsed state. Without it, the accordion’s state is ambiguous.

issuesFound.title: Icon‑only ThemeToggle button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <IconButton> used in ThemeToggle contains only an icon and does not provide an aria‑label, so screen‑reader users cannot know its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: `<IconButton aria-label="Toggle theme" onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` utility class sets `position: static` and `clip: auto`, which does not hide content visually while keeping it accessible. The correct technique requires `position: absolute`, `width: 1px`, `height: 1px`, `clip: rect(0 0 0 0)`, and `clip-path: inset(50%)`. This implementation fails WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) by not properly hiding content from sighted users while keeping it available to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img` and `.hero-image img:hover` rules apply a 3D transform animation (`transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(-3deg) rotateX(1deg)`) and box-shadow transition. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable or slow this motion, failing WCAG 2.3.3 (Animation from Interactions).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: The `.feature-card:hover` rule applies a transform (`transform: translateY(-2px)`) and box-shadow transition. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable this motion.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The `a { text-decoration: none; }` rule removes underlines from all links. No alternative visual differentiator (e.g., font-weight, border-bottom, background highlight on hover) is provided. Links are distinguished from surrounding text only by colour, failing WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 250: Remove `a { text-decoration: none; }` and ensure links have a non-colour visual indicator (e.g., underline, border-bottom, or background on hover/focus). Example:
    Line 250: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`
    Or provide alternative styles:
    Line 250: `a:hover, a:focus { text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.fix: Line 350: `html { font-size: 16px; }`

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onPointerDown` and `_onPointerUp` event handlers in the `testimonialsCarousel` module (lines 10234–10259) are attached to a non-native interactive element (`_rootEl`). These handlers respond to pointer events to implement swipe gestures, but the element lacks a `role` attribute and keyboard event handlers for `Enter` and `Space` keys. Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot trigger the swipe functionality, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10247: Add `role="region"` and `tabindex="0"` to `_rootEl` to make it focusable.
  Line 10248: Add a `keydown` event handler to `_rootEl` that listens for `Enter` and `Space` keys and triggers the same swipe logic as pointer events.

fpTitles: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module's `_onTriggerClick` function handles click events on the `.nav-toggle` element, but there is no corresponding keydown handler for Enter/Space keys. This prevents keyboard users from activating the mobile navigation menu.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module's `open` and `close` functions update the navigation state but do not announce these changes to screen readers via a live region. This prevents screen reader users from knowing the navigation menu's state.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module's `open` function toggles the navigation menu state but does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module's `init` function does not initialise the `aria-expanded` attribute on the navigation toggle button at page load.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` module's `_renderResult` function marks fields as invalid but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute to `true` for invalid fields.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` module's `_renderResult` function does not explicitly set `aria-invalid` to `false` for valid fields, relying on `srUtils.markValid` which may not update the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `cookieConsent` module's `_onAccept` and `_onDecline` functions toggle the consent state but do not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the accept/decline buttons.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle element (`.nav-toggle`) is enhanced with pointer event handlers but lacks matching keyboard handlers for Enter/Space. Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot activate the mobile menu.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions in `mobileNav` toggle the menu state but do not update `aria-expanded` on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the menu state.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions in `mobileNav` change the visible state of the navigation menu but do not announce the change to screen readers via a live region.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField()` function in `inlineValidator` marks fields as invalid but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field element.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField()` function in `inlineValidator` does not clear `aria-invalid` when a field becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init()` function in `mobileNav` does not initialise `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button for the closed state.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing role and tabindex
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle element (`.nav-toggle`) is not a native `<button>` and lacks `role="button"` and `tabindex="0"`, making it unreachable by keyboard and unannounced by screen readers.

issuesFound.title: aria-pressed not initialised on toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle button does not initialise `aria-pressed` to reflect its initial state.

issuesFound.title: aria-pressed not updated on toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick()` function in `pricing` toggles the billing period but does not update `aria-pressed` on the toggle button.

issuesFound.title: aria-controls missing on dropdown trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `register()` function in `dropdownMenu` does not set `aria-controls` on the trigger button to reference the panel id.

issuesFound.title: aria-controls missing on FAQ trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `parse()` function in `faqRegistry` does not set `aria-controls` on the trigger button to reference the panel id.

issuesFound.title: aria-labelledby missing on FAQ panel
issuesFound.explanation: The `parse()` function in `faqRegistry` does not set `aria-labelledby` on the panel to reference the trigger id.

issuesFound.title: aria-live region not injected for dynamic announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The `liveRegion` module is configured to inject a live region if not present (`CONFIG.accessibility.injectLiveRegion: true`), but the injection logic is missing from the `init()` function.

issuesFound.title: aria-live region not used for form submission status
issuesFound.explanation: The `_showSuccess()` and `_showError()` functions in `newsletterForm` update the status element but do not use the `liveRegion` module to announce the status to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: aria-live region not used for wizard step changes
issuesFound.explanation: The `_goToStep()` function in `multiStepWizard` changes the visible step but does not announce the step change to screen readers via a live region.

issuesFound.title: aria-live region not used for file upload status
issuesFound.explanation: The `_announce()` function in `fileUploadField` is not implemented; file upload status changes are not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 11800: function _announce(binding, message) {
      liveRegion.announce(message);

issuesFound.title: aria-atomic not set on live regions
issuesFound.explanation: The live region wrappers created in `liveRegion.init()` lack `aria-atomic="true"`, so screen readers may not re-read the entire message when it changes.

issuesFound.title: aria-relevant not set on live regions
issuesFound.explanation: The live region wrappers created in `liveRegion.init()` lack `aria-relevant="additions text"`, so screen readers may not reliably announce dynamic content.

issuesFound.title: aria-label missing on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input (`#search-input`) lacks an `aria-label` or associated `<label>`, so screen readers cannot announce its purpose.

issuesFound.title: aria-label missing on newsletter email input
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input lacks an `aria-label` or associated `<label>`.

issuesFound.title: aria-label missing on contact form inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form inputs lack `aria-label` or associated `<label>` elements.

issuesFound.title: aria-label missing on file upload zone
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload zone lacks an `aria-label` describing its purpose.

issuesFound.title: Decorative icon missing aria-hidden={true}
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon inside the NavDropdown trigger button is decorative but not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers will announce the arrow characters, which is redundant since the button's accessible name already conveys its state via aria-expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1302: `<span aria-hidden="true" className="nav-link__chevron">`

issuesFound.title: Required-field marker announces bare asterisk to AT — add aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The required-field marker (*) in the FormGroup component is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers will announce the asterisk as text, which is redundant since the input's required state is already conveyed via the required attribute or aria-required.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1900: ` *`

issuesFound.title: Icon-only interactive element missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle button contains only an icon (☀️/🌙/💻) and lacks an accessible name. Screen reader users will not understand the button's purpose or current state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1500: `<IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} aria-label={LABELS[theme]} className="theme-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: Toggle component trigger missing aria-expanded prop
issuesFound.explanation: The NavDropdown trigger button does not have an aria-expanded prop to indicate the dropdown's expanded/collapsed state. Screen reader users will not know whether the dropdown is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1300: `<button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>`

issuesFound.title: Form field missing aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component does not receive an aria-invalid prop when the field is in an error state. Screen reader users will not be informed that the input contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1950: `<input id={id} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: Interactive state not communicated to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The FilterTabs buttons do not receive an aria-pressed prop to indicate their active/inactive state. Screen reader users will not know which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200: `<button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>`

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component does not receive an aria-busy prop when in a loading state. Screen reader users will not be informed that the button is busy processing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850: `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger buttons (`item.trigger`) have inconsistent `aria-expanded` management. The `close()` function correctly sets `aria-expanded="false"`, but the `open()` function fails to set `aria-expanded="true"`. This leaves the state incorrect when an item is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2365: In `open()`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `item.open = true`.

issuesFound.title: pricing billing toggle does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing toggle switch (`_toggleEl`) does not update its `aria-checked` state (or `aria-pressed` if treated as a button). The `_onToggleClick()` function changes the period but does not update the ARIA attribute on the toggle element. The `init()` function does not set the initial state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2635: In `init()`, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, String(initPeriod === 'year'));` (assuming role="switch").
  Line 2760: In `_onToggleClick()`, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, String(next === 'year'));` after `setPeriod(next)`.
  Line 2745: In `_syncToggleState()`, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, String(period === 'year'));` if syncing from external change.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` module does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on fields when validation fails. The `_renderResult()` function calls `srUtils.markInvalid()`, which only manages `aria-describedby`. Screen reader users are not informed that the field value is invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3035: In `srUtils.markInvalid()`, add `control.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`.
  Line 3045: In `srUtils.markValid()`, add `control.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID);`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The product count badge (`_countBadge`) is updated dynamically via `_updateCountBadge()` when filters are applied, but the change is not announced to screen readers. The function updates `textContent` and `aria-label` but does not use a live region. Users relying on AT may not know the number of results has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2205: In `_updateCountBadge()`, add `liveRegion.announce(\`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found\`);` after updating the badge.

issuesFound.title: pricing savings message update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing savings message (`_savingsMsg`) is updated dynamically via `_updateSavingsMessage()` when the billing period changes, but the change is not announced to screen readers. The function updates `textContent` but does not use a live region. Users relying on AT may not hear the new savings information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2750: In `_updateSavingsMessage()`, add `liveRegion.announce(_savingsMsg.textContent);` after updating the text content.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator such as a box-shadow or border. This removes the default focus ring for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block sets outline: none on :focus-visible without a replacement. High contrast mode users rely on visible focus indicators; removing them entirely creates a critical barrier.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary button
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-primary` uses `background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary)` (#5588bb) with `color: var(--colour-text-on-brand)` (#ffffff). With the base font-size of 12px, this is normal text requiring 4.5:1 contrast. The actual ratio is approximately 3.73:1, failing WCAG AA.
issuesFound.fix: Line 654: Change to `background-color: #3a6fa3;` (or adjust --colour-brand-primary to #3a6fa3 or darker to achieve 4.5:1)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on danger button
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-danger` uses `background-color: var(--colour-error)` (#ee9999) with `color: var(--colour-neutral-0)` (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.09:1, failing both the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text and the 3:1 requirement for large text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 710: Change to `background-color: #dc2626;` (or adjust --colour-error to #dc2626 or darker)

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global selector `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes underlines from all links. Without an alternative visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or font-weight) in the default state, users cannot distinguish links from surrounding text except by color, failing WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 405: Add `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;` or remove the `text-decoration: none` rule to rely on browser defaults.

issuesFound.title: Base font size below recommended 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The `html` selector sets `font-size: 12px`, which is below the recommended 16px for body text. This reduces readability for users with low vision and overrides user agent defaults.
issuesFound.fix: Line 169: Change to `font-size: 16px;` or `font-size: 100%;` to respect user preferences.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class removes content from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, `height: auto`, and `overflow: visible`, which fails to hide content visually. It also lacks proper clipping (clip: rect), meaning content remains visible and may not be announced correctly by screen readers, or may be announced but clutter the layout.
issuesFound.fix: Line 427: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Broken sr-only utility class removes content from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, clip: auto, and overflow: visible, which keeps the content visible in the layout instead of hiding it visually while preserving it for screen readers. This defeats the purpose of the utility and may expose screen-reader-only text to all users or cause layout issues.
issuesFound.fix: Line 441-451: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Broken sr-only-focusable utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only-focusable class uses the same incorrect properties as .sr-only (position: static, clip: auto, etc.), failing to properly hide content visually while keeping it available to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 454-463: .sr-only-focusable { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border-color change. This removes the default focus ring from all interactive elements, making keyboard navigation impossible for sighted users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 404-408: :focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block removes the outline from :focus-visible without providing a high-contrast alternative, leaving users in forced-colors mode without any focus indication.
issuesFound.fix: Line 415-420: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Root font size too small for readability
issuesFound.explanation: Setting html font-size to 12px makes the default text size 12px (below 16px), which is smaller than the recommended minimum for body text and forces users to zoom to read comfortably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 94: html { font-size: 16px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line height too tight
issuesFound.explanation: The p selector sets line-height: 1, which is below the WCAG recommended 1.5 for body text. This reduces readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 128: p { line-height: var(--line-height-normal); ... }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with cognitive disabilities or dyslexia.
issuesFound.fix: Line 109: body { ... word-spacing: normal; ... }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative visual indicator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Without an alternative non-color indicator (such as border-bottom or font-weight), links are only distinguishable by color, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 135: a { text-decoration: underline; } or add border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override – .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transform var(--transition-fast)), which can cause motion sensitivity issues, but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override is provided to disable this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1167-1170: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override – .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transition: transform var(--transition-slower) and transform effects on hover, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 1167-1170 is empty and does not disable the transform.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1167-1170: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast – .stat-label
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label uses color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (30% white) against a gradient background of approximately #5588bb, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.1:1, below the required 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast – .newsletter-section paragraph
issuesFound.explanation: The .newsletter-section > p uses color: rgba(255,255,255,0.8) against the #5588bb gradient background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.8:1, below the required 4.5:1 for normal text (font-size: var(--font-size-base) = 16px).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1600-1604: .newsletter-section > p { color: rgba(255,255,255,0.95); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast – .newsletter-section input hint
issuesFound.explanation: The .newsletter-section .input-hint uses color: rgba(255,255,255,0.65) against the #5588bb gradient background, resulting in insufficient contrast for small text (font-size: var(--font-size-xs) = 12px).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1634: .newsletter-section .input-hint { color: rgba(255,255,255,0.9); }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2098: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error description from aria-describedby but does not remove or set aria-invalid="false" on the field, leaving it incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2108: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` after clearing aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications auto-dismiss without pause mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: The toastManager uses setTimeout to auto-dismiss notifications after a fixed duration without providing a way for users to pause, stop, or extend the timer. This violates WCAG 2.2.1 for users who need more time to read the notification.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1920: Replace the simple setTimeout with a pausable timer that clears on mouseenter/focusin and resets on mouseleave/focusout, or provide a persistent dismiss button that remains visible until explicitly activated.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the module initializes, leaving the attribute missing until the first toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2480 (in init function, after _trigger is assigned): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded updated when panels open or close. The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions manipulate panel visibility but omit the ARIA state on the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2530 (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` Line 2540 (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, they are not initialized with aria-expanded="false", leaving the attribute missing until interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2580 (in register function, after panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')): Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion trigger buttons do not update aria-expanded when items are opened or closed dynamically after page load. The init sets initial state, but open/close functions omit updates.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4250 (in open function): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` Line 4270 (in close function): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init missing aria-expanded initialization on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the suggestion list is hidden, violating the combobox pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2850 (in suggestionListRenderer.mount, after setting aria-controls): Add `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle uses role="switch" but _syncToggleState only updates CSS classes, not the required aria-checked attribute to indicate the on/off state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3750 (in _syncToggleState, inside the _toggleEl check): Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, String(isYear));`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-selected updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period selection buttons (month/quarter/year) function as tabs or toggle buttons but do not have aria-selected (for tabs) or aria-pressed updated to reflect the active selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3740 (in _syncToggleState, inside the _periodBtns forEach): Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.SELECTED, String(isActive));` (if using tab pattern) or `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` (if using button pattern).

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod not announcing price changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When billing periods change, prices update visually and via textContent, but there is no announcement via a live region to inform screen reader users of the new prices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3710 (in setPeriod, after updating plan cards): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingChanged || 'Prices updated to ${period} billing');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters not announcing results to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count changes and the empty state may appear, but these changes are not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that results have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3280 (in applyFilters, inside filterAnimation.run callback): Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` or similar announcement after the animation completes.

faqAccordion open/close functions missing aria-expanded updates

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded state update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function in dropdownMenu reveals the dropdown panel but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2050: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing aria-expanded state update
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function hides the dropdown panel but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "false". Assistive technologies cannot announce the collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2070: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded state update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the FAQ answer panel but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (the close function sets it to false, but open never sets it to true). Screen readers cannot convey the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3150: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: pricing._syncToggleState() missing aria-pressed state update
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module's billing period toggle buttons do not have their aria-pressed attribute updated to reflect the active/pressed state. Screen reader users cannot identify which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3050: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator._renderResult() missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validation function marks fields as invalid by adding CSS classes and error messages but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or clear aria-invalid on valid fields. Screen readers cannot convey the validation state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3750: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, result.valid ? 'false' : 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the navigation toggle button when the menu is closed on page load. Screen readers may not recognize the button as controlling an expandable region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1830: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch.init() missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init() function configures the search input as a combobox but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" for the initially closed suggestion list. Screen readers may not recognize the expandable nature of the control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2700: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function updates the product grid and count badge but does not announce the filter results (e.g., "Showing 5 products") via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not notified that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2850: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod function updates pricing display and billing labels but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when prices switch between monthly and annual billing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3020: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop._onClick() missing completion announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button scrolls the page and moves focus but does not announce the action completion to screen readers. Users may not realize the page has scrolled to the top.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2280: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');

fpTitles: scrollToTop._onClick() missing completion announcement

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on page load, nor does it update the attribute to "true" when the menu opens or "false" when it closes. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded/collapsed state of the navigation drawer.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1900 (in open): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
  Line 1920 (in close): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`
  Line 1970 (in init): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the markInvalid function adds the error element to aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers do not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1250 (in markInvalid, after addDescribedBy): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation errors, the markValid function removes the error reference from aria-describedby but does not remove aria-invalid or set it to "false", leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1260 (in markValid, after removing describedby): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the state change is not announced to screen readers via the live region utility. Users do not receive auditory feedback that the accordion state has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3400 (in open, after emit): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.title));`
  Line 3420 (in close, after emit): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.title));`

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied to the product grid, the updated results count is not announced to screen readers. Users do not know that the grid has updated or how many products are now showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3050 (in applyFilters callback): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory));`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price updates are not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize that the displayed prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2750 (in setPeriod): Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: Contact form inputs missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form inputs for full name and email lack autocomplete attributes, preventing browsers from autofilling known values and assistive technologies from programmatically identifying the purpose of the fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4500 (after field selection): Add `qs('[name="full_name"]', _formEl).setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');` and `qs('[name="email"]', _formEl).setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input lacks the autocomplete="email" attribute, preventing proper browser autofill and assistive technology identification.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4400 (after _emailEl = ...): Add `_emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not trapped within the menu, allowing users to tab behind the overlay. The _trap variable is declared but never initialized or activated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1900 (in open): Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer); _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus not returned on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it, disorienting keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1920 (in close, before emit): Add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: Search clear button may lack accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search clear button is selected from the DOM but the code does not ensure it has an accessible name via aria-label. If the button contains only an "×" icon, screen readers will not announce its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2300 (after _clearBtn = ...): Add `if (_clearBtn && !_clearBtn.getAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(_clearBtn, ARIA.LABEL, STRINGS.labels.clearSearch || 'Clear search');`

issuesFound.title: Base font size set below 16px impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, making 1rem equal to 12px. Body text using rem units will render below the recommended 16px minimum, impairing readability for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 45: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line-height set to 1 impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The p element has line-height: 1, which is below the recommended 1.5 minimum. Tight line spacing makes text blocks harder to read, especially for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 111: line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed);

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces spacing between words. Negative word-spacing compresses text and impairs readability, particularly for users with reading disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 52: (remove the word-spacing property or set it to normal)

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without non-colour visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The a element has text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Links are distinguished from surrounding text by colour alone, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Colour for users who cannot perceive colour differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 108: a { text-decoration: underline; } (or add border-bottom or background highlight on :hover/focus states)

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24px. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate small buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, both below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24px. Icon-only buttons are particularly difficult for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] sets width: 28px and height: 28px with no padding, resulting in a touch target below the recommended 44×44px. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating the button.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element has a transform transition but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable or reduce the motion. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from animated transitions.
issuesFound.fix: Add inside the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block: .skip-link { transition: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn element has a transform transition but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable the motion. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from animated button interactions.
issuesFound.fix: Add inside the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block: .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation has empty prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img element has a transform transition, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block contains empty rules that do not disable the motion. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the 3D transform animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 732-737: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not set aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.fix: Line 2820: function open(announceToSR) { if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return; _open = true; addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen); setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); if (_overlay) { _overlay.removeAttribute('hidden'); requestAnimationFrame(() => addClass(_overlay, CSS.visible)); } _lockScroll(); emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer }); }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not set aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot tell that the navigation menu has been closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2850: function close(announceToSR) { if (!_open) return; _open = false; removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); removeClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen); setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); if (_overlay) { removeClass(_overlay, CSS.visible); const dur = parseInt(getCssVar('--transition-base', document.documentElement) || '250', 10); setTimeout(() => { if (_overlay) _overlay.setAttribute('hidden', ''); }, dur + 50); } _unlockScroll(); if (_trap && _trap.isActive()) _trap.deactivate(); emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer }); }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() does not set aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function links an error message to a field via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen reader users are not notified that the field contains invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2100: function markInvalid(field, errorEl) { if (!isElement(field) || !isElement(errorEl)) return; field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error message reference but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. Screen reader users may still hear the field announced as invalid even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2115: function markValid(field, errorEl) { if (!isElement(field)) return; field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); if (isElement(errorEl) && errorEl.id) { const refs = (field.getAttribute('aria-describedby') || '').split(/\s+/).filter(id => id && id !== errorEl.id); if (refs.length) { field.setAttribute('aria-describedby', refs.join(' ')); } else { field.removeAttribute('aria-describedby'); } } }

(STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing — save 20%') : (STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing')); emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period }); }

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav close function does not update aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component for closing the mobile navigation has no aria-label prop. The IconButton interface requires aria-label for icon-only buttons, but this instance omits it. Screen reader users would hear no accessible name.

```tsx

>

</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav renders a link with className 'nav-link--current' for the current page, but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify the current page link.

issuesFound.title: aria-modal attribute placed on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal-overlay div, but it should be on the element with role="dialog". This causes screen readers to incorrectly interpret the modal's boundaries.

issuesFound.title: Carousel slides not hidden from assistive technology when inactive
issuesFound.explanation: Non-visible testimonial slides lack aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users may encounter all slides in the DOM simultaneously, causing confusion about which content is currently displayed.

issuesFound.title: Decorative cloned content not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated integration logos list for seamless visual looping lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users will encounter the same content twice.

  • {item.name}
    • ))}
```

issuesFound.title: Decorative star characters not hidden from assistive technology

issuesFound.fix: Line 368-377: ```tsx {stars.map((filled, i) => ( {filled ? '★' : '☆'} ))} ```

issuesFound.title: Decorative spinner element not hidden from assistive technology

issuesFound.title: Decorative spinner ring not hidden from assistive technology

issuesFound.title: Navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks nav element has no aria-label or aria-labelledby. When multiple nav elements exist on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish this navigation region from others.

issuesFound.title: Section component accepts labelledby prop but never applies it
issuesFound.explanation: The Section component accepts a labelledby prop intended for aria-labelledby, but the prop is never applied to the rendered element. Sections using labelledby will not have their accessible names properly associated.

issuesFound.title: Statistic value missing accessible name for complex formatted content
issuesFound.explanation: The StatItem component renders formatted values like "40,000+" and "99.9%" without providing an accessible name. Screen readers may announce these inconsistently. The valueLabel property exists in the Stat interface but is never used.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: When the Button is in loading state (isLoading=true), it does not communicate aria-busy="true" to assistive technology. Screen reader users are not informed that the button is currently busy processing.

issuesFound.title: Disclosure button missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The NavDropdown trigger button has aria-controls and aria-haspopup but is missing aria-expanded. Screen reader users cannot tell whether the dropdown menu is open or closed, which is a WCAG 4.1.2 violation for disclosure components.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button has aria-controls but is missing aria-expanded. Screen reader users cannot tell whether the answer panel is expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal placed on overlay instead of dialog
issuesFound.explanation: aria-modal="true" is placed on the modal-overlay div, but it should be on the element with role="dialog". Screen readers may not correctly suppress background content.

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle icon button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop, but ThemeToggle does not provide one. The button contains only an emoji icon with no accessible name, making it impossible for screen reader users to understand its purpose.

issuesFound.title: Carousel navigation buttons missing accessible names

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation link uses a CSS class to indicate the current page but does not expose this state programmatically via aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify which page they are currently on.

issuesFound.title: Active mobile navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation link uses a CSS class to indicate the current page but does not expose this state programmatically via aria-current="page".

issuesFound.title: Hero section missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: Stats section missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: Skip navigation landmark missing accessible name
