issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav nav element has an id but no aria-label or aria-labelledby. When multiple nav elements exist, each needs an accessible name for screen reader users to distinguish between them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 694: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner span inside the loading button is decorative (the accessible name comes from VisuallyHidden) but is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers may announce meaningless spinner content.

issuesFound.title: Spinner component ring missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner__ring span is purely decorative but not hidden from assistive technology. The accessible name comes from aria-label and VisuallyHidden, so the ring should be aria-hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 640: <span className="spinner__ring" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: Star rating decorative stars missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star spans (★ and ☆) are decorative since the parent already has aria-label providing the rating. Without aria-hidden, screen readers may announce each star character redundantly.

issuesFound.title: Hidden carousel slides missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Non-visible carousel slides do not have aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users may encounter all slides in reading order rather than just the current slide, causing confusion.

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component does not communicate its loading state to assistive technology via aria-busy. Screen reader users are not informed that the button is currently processing.

issuesFound.title: Required field asterisk missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The required field asterisk (*) is a visual indicator that may be announced as "star" or "asterisk" by screen readers. It should be hidden from assistive technology since the VisuallyHidden "(required)" text already conveys this information.

fpTitles: ThemeToggle icon button missing accessible name
Desktop navigation landmark missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation close IconButton renders only a visual icon (✕) without an aria-label prop. The IconButton interface requires aria-label for icon-only buttons, but this instance omits it, leaving the button without an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 729: aria-label="Close navigation menu"

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav renders current page links with className 'nav-link--current' but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify the current page in navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 707: aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component has an isLoading state but does not apply aria-busy={isLoading}. Screen reader users are not informed when a button is in a loading/busy state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 420: aria-busy={isLoading || undefined}

issuesFound.title: Required field marker exposes raw asterisk to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The required field asterisk span ( *) lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may announce "asterisk" or "*" redundantly since VisuallyHidden already provides "(required)".
issuesFound.fix: Line 533: *

issuesFound.title: aria-modal attribute placed on overlay instead of dialog
issuesFound.explanation: The Modal component places aria-modal="true" on the overlay div, but aria-modal belongs on the element with role="dialog" (the inner modal-dialog div). This misplacement may confuse assistive technology about which element is the modal container.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1528: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>
  Fix: Line 1534: aria-modal="true"

issuesFound.title: Form field error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The NewsletterForm email input conditionally references aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" when there's an error, but no element with that ID exists in the document. Screen reader users cannot access the error message programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1445: {email.state.touched && email.state.error && {email.state.error}}

issuesFound.title: Hidden carousel slides not marked with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Non-visible testimonial slides lack aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users may navigate to hidden slides that are visually off-screen, causing confusion about carousel content and position.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1331: aria-hidden={!isVisible || undefined}

issuesFound.title: Statistic value missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The StatItem component renders stat.value (e.g., "40,000+") without an accessible label. The Stat interface defines valueLabel ("40,000 plus") for screen readers, but it is never used. Complex formatted values may be mispronounced by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 880:

{stat.value}

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The btn-spinner span inside the loading button has no aria-hidden="true". Although a VisuallyHidden loading label is provided, the decorative spinner element should be explicitly hidden from assistive technology to prevent any potential redundant announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 422: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />
